title: Make it switch
intro: Welcome to the fab. This planar transistor's gate length has been shrunk so hard it barely turns off — the drain is stealing control of the channel. Drag the gate length and watch the Id–Vg curve until the device actually switches: at least 10,000× more current ON than OFF.
explain: You just met short-channel effects. Below ~30 nm, a planar gate loses electrostatic control: the off-current floor rises exponentially and the on/off ratio collapses. Real logic devices target Ion/Ioff of 10⁴–10⁶. And a fun fact about names: at the real "5nm" node the gate length is ~18 nm (IRDS 2021) — node names stopped meaning physical dimensions around 2011. You needed a much longer gate than the marketing implies.
